organic-the-bank-kept-47-of-my-mom-s-bir.hook: The bank kept $47 of my mom's birthday money. Never again. 😤

payload.outline: Beat 1 (0-8s): Creator holds up phone showing a bank wire receipt with fees circled in red — emotional, personal. Beat 2 (9-25s): Screen-record of opening Speed Wallet, entering the same dollar amount to send internationally, fee line reads $0.00. Beat 3 (26-45s): Recipient's phone buzzes — show notification arriving in under 5 seconds, both parties react on split screen. Beat 4 (46-58s): Creator to camera: 'Zero fees, instant, on Bitcoin Lightning. Link in bio to send your first $5 free.'
payload.production_notes: Film fee receipt close-up in natural light. Use phone mirror trick for split-screen reaction. No caption cards needed — spoken audio carries the narrative. Post Tuesday 6-8 PM EST when diaspora engagement peaks.

organic-pov-you-just-found-bitcoin-in-20.hook: POV: you just found Bitcoin in 2009 — except it's 2025 and Lightning is the unlock 🔓

payload.outline: Beat 1 (0-7s): Text-on-screen over retro Bitcoin price chart: '$0.001 in 2009 → $60K today. You missed it. Or did you?' Beat 2 (8-25s): Pivot — 'Lightning Network is Bitcoin payments finally working: instant, global, nearly free. This is the 2009 moment for spending BTC.' Beat 3 (26-45s): Speed Wallet screen-record: receive sats, pay a Lightning invoice, whole flow under 10 seconds. Beat 4 (46-58s): 'Download Speed. Send $1 anywhere on earth right now. This is your early-adopter window.'
payload.production_notes: Use free CapCut retro-glitch transition between chart and app demo. Source public-domain BTC price chart imagery. Trending lo-fi nostalgia audio underneath. Hook text must appear within first 0.5s to stop the scroll.

organic-they-charged-filipinos-2-4b-in-r.hook: They charged Filipinos $2.4B in remittance fees last year. Here's the receipts. 🧾

payload.outline: Beat 1 (0-10s): Talking head — creator visibly frustrated, cites the OFW fee stat from hook #4 energy, references real World Bank data on-screen as text overlay. Beat 2 (11-28s): 'Western Union, MoneyGram, your bank — all taking 5-10% off every send. That's money that should feed families.' Beat 3 (29-48s): 'Speed Wallet uses Bitcoin Lightning. $0 fees. Arrives in seconds. I'm going to show you exactly how.' Screen-record demo. Beat 4 (49-58s): 'Share this with every OFW you know. Comment your country and I'll show the fee math for your corridor.'
payload.production_notes: Tag #OFW #Philippines #remittance #pinoy in caption for algorithmic targeting. Creator should speak directly and sincerely — no heavy production. World Bank 2024 remittance cost data is publicly available. Engagement bait in Beat 4 drives comment velocity.

organic-i-sent-bitcoin-to-my-friend-in-n.hook: I sent Bitcoin to my friend in Nigeria. It arrived before I finished this sentence. ⚡

payload.outline: Beat 1 (0-6s): Creator presses send on Speed Wallet, starts speaking mid-sentence — recipient's push notification appears on second phone before sentence ends. Real-time proof. Beat 2 (7-22s): 'Traditional wire: 3-5 days, $25-45 in fees. Speed on Lightning: 2 seconds, zero fees. Both of us just needed a phone.' Beat 3 (23-42s): Show both sides — sender's Speed balance deducted, receiver's balance credited. Both give thumbs up to camera. Beat 4 (43-58s): 'Download Speed Wallet free. Your first send is on us.'
payload.production_notes: Coordinate with an actual friend or creator in Nigeria/Ghana/Philippines for authentic dual-phone footage. Film both phones simultaneously using a tripod or second person. The real-time arrival is the entire product demo — protect that moment, do not cut away.

organic-your-first-bitcoin-doesn-t-have-.hook: Your first Bitcoin doesn't have to be complicated. It's literally just this. 👇

payload.outline: Beat 1 (0-8s): Creator admits on camera: 'I was scared of crypto for 3 years because it looked too technical. Then someone showed me Speed Wallet and I got it in 60 seconds.' Beat 2 (9-28s): Screen-record: download app, create wallet, tap 'Receive,' share QR code. Friend sends $2 in sats. Whole flow shown unedited. Beat 3 (29-46s): 'That's it. You now own Bitcoin. No exchange account, no KYC drama, no fees. Just a phone.' Beat 4 (47-58s): 'Comment SATS and I'll send you $1 in Bitcoin to your new Speed wallet right now.'
payload.production_notes: The comment-to-receive mechanic in Beat 4 is a proven engagement multiplier — budget $20-50 to actually follow through and send sats to commenters, driving authentic social proof replies. Screen-record at 2x speed for the setup, then slow to real-time for the sats arriving.

paid-your-family-s-money-is-being-stolen.hook: Your family's money is being stolen legally. Every. Single. Send.
paid-your-family-s-money-is-being-stolen.estimate: $1.80-$2.60 (fear-of-loss + diaspora identity targeting typically beats benchmark by 15-40%; strong problem/solution narrative should compress CPI below $3.17)

payload.problem: B-roll of a wire transfer receipt with fees highlighted — VO: 'The average remittance corridor charges 6.2%. On a $300 send, that's $18.60 gone. Every month. That's $223 a year your family never sees. Western Union keeps it. Your bank keeps it. Nobody tells you there's a zero-fee option.'
payload.solution: Speed Wallet screen-record — VO: 'Speed uses Bitcoin Lightning Network. Send $300 to the Philippines, Mexico, Nigeria — zero fees, arrives in under 5 seconds. Your family gets every dollar.' Show push notification arriving. Show $0.00 fee line clearly.
payload.audience: Meta (Facebook + Instagram): ages 25-45, interests = international money transfer, remittances, Filipino diaspora, Nigerian diaspora, Latino immigrant communities, Western Union, MoneyGram; household income under $75K; US only

paid-what-if-your-first-bitcoin-took-30-.hook: What if your first Bitcoin took 30 seconds?
paid-what-if-your-first-bitcoin-took-30-.estimate: $2.40-$3.10 (curiosity-gap hook with low-friction CTA should approach benchmark; TikTok's auction efficiency for fintech has improved; test UGC vs produced versions to find floor)

payload.problem: Talking head of relatable 25-35yo — 'I wanted to try Bitcoin for years but every app felt like filing taxes. Coinbase wanted my passport. Binance had 14 screens. I gave up three times.' Cut to confused face scrolling complex exchange UI.
payload.solution: Speed Wallet screen-record with upbeat audio: download → create wallet → receive QR code → $2 in sats arrives. VO: 'Speed Wallet. No exchange. No ID verification for small amounts. Just download, get your wallet address, and your first sats can arrive in the next 60 seconds. Bitcoin, finally simple.'
payload.audience: TikTok Ads: ages 21-35, interest clusters = cryptocurrency beginner, personal finance, fintech apps, Cash App users, PayPal users; lookalike from crypto-curious content engagers; US + Canada

paid-casino-cashout-in-2-seconds-not-2-d.hook: Casino cashout in 2 seconds. Not 2 days.
paid-casino-cashout-in-2-seconds-not-2-d.estimate: $3.00-$4.20 (iGaming audience CPMs run higher due to regulatory pressure on targeting; strong utility hook should hold near benchmark — test $3.17 cap with autobid and expand if ROAS supports; expect efficiency gains as pixel trains on depositor LTV)

payload.problem: Screen-record of a generic sportsbook/casino withdrawal page showing '3-5 business days processing' — VO: 'You won. Congrats. Now wait 5 days for your money while they hold it. ACH delays, card reversals, random 'verification holds' — it's your money and they act like it's theirs.'
payload.solution: Speed Wallet Lightning deposit/withdrawal flow — VO: 'Speed Wallet on Bitcoin Lightning. Deposit to any Lightning-enabled casino instantly. Cash out and see your balance update in under 3 seconds. No holds. No reversals. No waiting. Provably instant.' Show timestamp on withdrawal vs balance update — same second.
payload.audience: Meta (Facebook + Instagram): ages 21-45, interests = online sports betting, DraftKings, FanDuel, online poker, casino games, cryptocurrency payments; male skew 70/30; US states where iGaming is legal (NJ, PA, MI, CO, NV, IL); exclude problem-gambling sensitive audiences per Meta policy
